notes: Greek philosopher, successor to Aristotle as head of the Peripatetic school, known for his works on ethics and natural philosophy.

docs.title: Letter XVI
docs.author: Marcus Tullius Cicero
docs.excerpt: … to a practical life, Theophrastus to a contemplative, I have set my mind on making it clear that I have humoured them both. I take it I have fully satisfied Dicaearchus: now I am turning my eye to the other school, which not only gives me permission to take my ease now, but blames me for ever
